source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service worker nodes are updated to the latest image to ensure patching of vulnerabilities

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Ensure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service worker nodes are updated to the latest image to ensure patching of vulnerabilities
input_row.description: Update the worker nodes in a cluster to the latest patch version so that security fixes are applied to those worker nodes.
input_row.rationale: As security updates and patches are put in place for the API server and other master components, you must be sure that the worker nodes remain in sync. You can make two types of updates: updating only the patch version, or updating the major.minor version with the patch version. This benchmark is only in relation to updating the patch version, not the major or minor versions. Updating the patch version in a timely manner ensures patching of known vulnerabilities and will keep your worker nodes more secure. Impact: The worker node update also updates the worker node to the same major.minor version as the master if a major.minor update is also available.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console. 2. To view a list of your resources, go to Menu > Resource List . 3. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your cluster. 4. Select the Worker Nodes tab. 5. For each listed worker node, ensure that next to the Version no information icon is present indicating that an update is available. If there is an information icon indicating there is an update available, then the worker node is not running the latest available image. From Command Line: 1. List the clusters in your IBM Cloud account ibmcloud ks cluster ls  2. List the workers for a given cluster. ibmcloud ks workers -c <cluster_name_or_ID> 3. Ensure that next to the version for a worker node no asterisk (*) is present indicating that an update is available. a. Here is an example output that shows that there is an available update for a worker node ibmcloud ks workers -c mykubecluster-free OK ID                                                       Public IP Private IP      Flavor   State    Status   Zone    Version kube-btdsgk5d0t1lpluh7f40-mykubeclust-default-000000d1   173.193.100.98 10.76.216.200   free     normal   Ready    hou02   1.17.11_1538* * To update to 1.17.12_1541 version, run 'ibmcloud ks worker update'. Review and make any required version changes before you update: 'https://ibm.biz/upworker' b. Here is an example output showing there are no available updates for a worker node ibmcloud ks workers -c mykubecluster-free OK ID                                                       Public IP Private IP      Flavor   State    Status   Zone    Version kube-btdsgk5d0t1lpluh7f40-mykubeclust-default-000000d1   173.193.100.98 10.76.216.200   free     normal   Ready    hou02   1.17.12_1541
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Log in to the IBM Cloud console. 2. Optional: Add capacity to your cluster by resizing the worker pool. The pods on the worker node can be rescheduled and continue running on the added worker nodes during the update. 3. To view a list of your resources, go to Menu > Resource List . 4. From your IBM Cloud resource list, select your cluster. 5. Select the Worker Nodes tab. 6. Select the checkbox for each worker node that you want to update. An action bar is displayed over the table header row. 7. From the action bar, click Update . From Command Line: 1. Complete the prerequisite steps. 2. Optional: Add capacity to your cluster by resizing the worker pool. The pods on the worker node can be rescheduled and continue running on the added worker nodes during the update. 3. List the worker nodes in your cluster and note the ID and Primary IP of the worker node that you want to update. ibmcloud ks worker ls --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID> 4. Replace the worker node to update the worker node to the latest patch version at the same major.minor version. ibmcloud ks worker replace --cluster <cluster_name_or_ID> --worker <worker_node_ID> 5. Repeat these steps for each worker node that you want to update. 6. Optional: After the replaced worker nodes are in a Ready status, resize the worker pool to meet the cluster capacity that you want. Default Value: Worker nodes are not updated unless the user initiates an update. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers- update#vpc_worker_node 2. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers- cs_versions#update_types 3. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers- cs_versions#version_types
input_row.impact: The worker node update also updates the worker node to the same major.minor version as the master if a major.minor update is also available.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control involves checking the version status of IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service worker nodes to ensure they are updated to the latest patch version. The details provided in the audit section clearly describe the use of IBM Cloud CLI commands to list clusters and worker nodes and to check their version status. The presence of indicators (such as an asterisk in the command-line output) that signify available updates can be programmatically determined using IBM Cloud APIs and CLI, indicating the suitability for automation.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_kubernetes_cluster_worker_nodes_patch_version_compliance

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **IBM Cloud CLI Command:**
  - Use `ibmcloud ks cluster ls` to list all clusters in the account.
  - Use `ibmcloud ks workers -c <cluster_name_or_ID>` to list all worker nodes for each cluster, displaying their versions.
- **Configuration Properties to Validate:**
  - Check each worker node for a version indicator (such as an asterisk) which signifies that an update is available.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:**
  - **Pass:** No worker nodes have an update indicator (asterisk next to the version), meaning they are on the latest patch version.
  - **Fail:** One or more worker nodes have an update indicator (asterisk next to the version), indicating they are not on the latest patch version.
- **References:**
  - Utilize the official IBM Cloud Kubernetes documentation for any additional command details: [IBM Cloud Kubernetes Versions](https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/containers?topic=containers-cs_versions#update_types)
- **Expected Values:**
  - All worker nodes show the latest available patch version without update indicators.

By leveraging the IBM Cloud CLI for these checks, the entire process can be automated, allowing large-scale auditing of clusters efficiently and effectively.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The use of IBM Cloud CLI commands to check the version status of Kubernetes worker nodes is appropriate for automation. The IBM Cloud CLI and APIs provide the necessary capabilities to perform these checks programmatically, making the automated approach suitable.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The IBM Cloud CLI commands `ibmcloud ks cluster ls` and `ibmcloud ks workers -c <cluster_name_or_ID>` are valid for listing clusters and worker nodes, respectively. The description of using an asterisk to indicate available updates is correct based on typical CLI output.
- The program name `ibm_kubernetes_cluster_worker_nodes_patch_version_compliance` is descriptive but could be more concise. It follows the naming convention but could be improved for clarity.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The program name could be improved to `ibm_kubernetes_worker_nodes_patch_compliance` for brevity and clarity.
- Consider including a step to automatically trigger updates for non-compliant nodes, if feasible, to enhance the automation process.
- It might be beneficial to incorporate IBM Cloud SDK capabilities for more advanced automation and integration into broader compliance frameworks.
- Ensure that the script handles API rate limits and errors gracefully, providing retry mechanisms or alerts as needed.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-founded, and the use of IBM Cloud CLI for automation is appropriate. The technical details are largely accurate, and the approach aligns with IBM Cloud capabilities.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Update the program name to `ibm_kubernetes_worker_nodes_patch_compliance` for improved clarity and conciseness. Consider adding steps for automatic remediation and error handling to enhance the automation process.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:**  
Both AI opinions correctly identify that the IBM Cloud CLI and APIs provide the necessary capabilities to programmatically check the patch version status of Kubernetes Service worker nodes. The audit steps can be fully automated by querying cluster and worker node versions and checking for update indicators (such as an asterisk in CLI output or corresponding API fields). No manual inspection or console-only steps are required, and all relevant properties are exposed via IBM Cloud CLI and APIs.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:**  
ibm_kubernetes_worker_nodes_patch_version_compliance

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- IBM Cloud Resource/Service: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) worker nodes
- API/CLI Method:  
  - List clusters: `ibmcloud ks cluster ls`
  - List worker nodes for each cluster: `ibmcloud ks workers -c <cluster_name_or_ID>`
- Properties to Check:  
  - For each worker node, check the `Version` field for an update indicator (e.g., an asterisk in CLI output or equivalent API property indicating an available patch update).
- Pass Criteria:  
  - All worker nodes are on the latest patch version (no update indicator present).
- Fail Criteria:  
  - One or more worker nodes have an update indicator (e.g., an asterisk), meaning they are not on the latest patch version.

---

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:**  
There was no substantive disagreement between the AI opinions; both supported automation. The second opinion suggested a slightly more concise program name, which has been adopted for clarity and alignment with IBM naming conventions.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
